title: Reconcile statement evidence to a Canonical Transaction
preconditions: The statement row was parsed and approved outside LEDGER; the account and statement scope are valid; the caller supplies evidence identity, candidate facts, deterministic-policy identity, and Idempotency Identity.
postconditions: Each statement row has at most one active economic effect; the statement governs the reconciled financial facts; prior agent capture remains auditable; exact matches do not duplicate; corrections leave one active replacement; no-match rows become statement-only only when authorized; ambiguity remains unapplied.
main_flow: 1. INGEST reads an approved statement outside LEDGER, registers or reuses privacy-safe statement-row evidence, and requests compatible unreconciled candidates. 2. The caller submits one closed disposition, authoritative statement facts, policy or explicit owner authority, match basis, reason, and Idempotency Identity. 3. Tally independently validates evidence, economic-event compatibility, uniqueness, policy, lifecycle, and current links. 4. Exact facts produce one confirming link to the existing transaction. 5. A compatible prior event with differing facts produces one statement-derived replacement, supersession, decision, link, and explicit category/pool/payment carry-forward in one transaction. 6. No compatible prior event may create one Statement-only Transaction. 7. Multiple candidates remain ambiguous and conflicts become exceptions without guessed financial effects. 8. Tally returns the durable state and exact current-effect reference.
alt_flows: 1a. Statement evidence replay is identical -> return the existing outcome with no duplicate effect
2a. Policy, owner authority, reason, or match basis is missing -> reject the requested application
3a. Candidate is inactive, evidence is confirmed elsewhere, or replacement facts violate Ledger invariants -> return an exception without changing the active financial effect
4a. More than one compatible or guard candidate exists -> return ambiguous and do not guess
5a. Replacement, supersession, carry-forward, decision, or evidence link cannot commit atomically -> commit none of them
6a. Statement-only creation fails -> commit neither transaction nor evidence link
7a. Same key requests a different outcome -> return conflict and preserve the original
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"INGEST submits one accepted statement-row evidence record and its approved statement scope for reconciliation.","status":"draft"}
